name: The Living World
full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > The Living World

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > The Living World

questions.question: Taxon is a:
questions.question_raw: Taxon is a:
questions.explanation: Each category in the taxonomic hierarchy represents the rank and is commonly termed as a taxon. Hence, the correct option is Ranking.

questions.question: The expansion of ICBN is:
questions.question_raw: The expansion of ICBN is:
questions.explanation: The scientific names are based on agreed principles and criteria, which are provided in the International Code for Botanical Nomenclature (ICBN). Hence, the correct option is the International code of botanical nomenclature.

questions.question: The expansion of ICZN is:
questions.question_raw: The expansion of ICZN is:
questions.explanation: Animal taxonomists have evolved the International Code of Zoological Nomenclature (ICZN). Hence, the correct option is the International code of zoological nomenclature.

questions.question: A binomial nomenclature has ________ words.
questions.question_raw: A binomial nomenclature has ________ words.
questions.explanation: The first word in a biological name represents the genus while the second component denotes the specific epithet. Hence, the correct option is Two.

questions.question: In Magnifera indica Linn, Linn denotes:
questions.question_raw: In Magnifera indica Linn, Linn denotes:

options.label: A
options.text: The name of the author who first described the formation

options.label: B
options.text: The name of the author who first described the species.

options.label: C
options.text: The name of the author who first described the genus.

options.label: D
options.text: The name of the author who first described the subspecies.

correct_option_texts: The name of the author who first described the species.
questions.explanation: In Mangifera indica Linn, the word ‘Linn’ indicates that this species was first described by Linnaeus. Hence, the correct option is the name of the author who first described the species.

questions.question: The scientific name of organisms should be written as:
questions.question_raw: The scientific name of organisms should be written as:
questions.explanation: Mangifera Indica. Here, Mangifera is a generic name while Indica is a specific name. Hence, the correct option is the Genus species.

questions.question: International Code of Biological Nomenclature is applied to_______.
questions.question_raw: International Code of Biological Nomenclature is applied to_______.
questions.explanation: Since biology deals with both plants and animals, the international code of biological nomenclature is applied to both plants and animals. Hence, the correct option is Both plant and animals.

questions.question: The first step in taxonomy is ______.
questions.question_raw: The first step in taxonomy is ______.
questions.explanation: The basics of taxonomy are the identification, naming, and classification of organisms are universally evolved under international codes. Hence, the correct option is Identification.

questions.question: Which one is the exclusive characteristic of a living organism?
questions.question_raw: Which one is the exclusive characteristic of a living organism?

options.label: A
options.text: Isolated metabolic reaction occuring in vitro.

options.label: B
options.text: Perception of events happening in the environment and their memory

options.label: C
options.text: Increase in mass both from outside and inside

correct_option_texts: Perception of events happening in the environment and their memory
questions.explanation: The most obvious and technically complicated feature of all living organisms is this ability to sense their surroundings or environment and respond to these environmental stimuli which could be physical, chemical or biological. We sense our environment through our sense organs. Plants respond to external factors like light, water, temperature, other organisms, pollutants, etc. All organisms, from the prokaryotes to the most complex eukaryotes can sense and respond to environmental cues. Hence, the correct option is the Perception of events happening in the environment and their memory.

questions.question: Living things differ from non-living things in a manner that:
questions.question_raw: Living things differ from non-living things in a manner that:

options.label: A
options.text: Living things respond to the physical level

options.label: B
options.text: Living things have a different level of organisation

options.label: C
options.text: Living things are more in member than non-living

correct_option_texts: Living things have a different level of organisation
questions.explanation: The cellular level organisation of the body is the defining feature of life forms or living things. Hence, the correct option is Living things have a different level of organisation.

questions.question:
Sometimes different authors give different names to one and the same species. In such a situation:
 The name under which the species was first described is a valid name.
 The latest name under which the species was described is valid.
 People can use any one of the names for the species.
 All known names are discarded and a new name is given to the species.
questions.question_raw:
Sometimes different authors give different names to one and the same species. In such a situation:

  1. The name under which the species was first described is a valid name.
  2. The latest name under which the species was described is valid.
  3. People can use any one of the names for the species.
  4. All known names are discarded and a new name is given to the species.
questions.explanation: In Mangifera indica Linn, the word ‘Linn’ indicates that this species was first described by Linnaeus. Hence, the correct option is The name under which the species was first described is the valid name.

questions.question: Hierarchical classification refers:
questions.question_raw: Hierarchical classification refers:

options.label: D
options.text: To rank one above the other

correct_option_texts: To rank one above the other
questions.explanation: Classification is not a single step process but involves a hierarchy of steps in which each step represents a rank or category. Hence, the correct option is To rank one above the other.

questions.question:
Which of the following statement is correct with respect to nomenclature?
 The scientific name when typed or handwritten should be underlined.
 Generic names always begin with capital letters whereas specific names begin with small letters.
 Scientific names should be printed in italics.
questions.question_raw:
Which of the following statement is correct with respect to nomenclature?

  1. The scientific name when typed or handwritten should be underlined.
  2. Generic names always begin with capital letters whereas specific names begin with small letters.
  3. Scientific names should be printed in italics.
questions.explanation: All of these are the universal rules of binomial nomenclature. Hence, the correct option is All of these.

questions.question: In a scientific name, the name of the author is printed in ________.
questions.question_raw: In a scientific name, the name of the author is printed in ________.
questions.explanation: The name of the author appears after the specific epithet, i.e., at the end of the biological name and is written in an abbreviated form, e.g., Mangifera indica Linn. It indicates that this species was first described by Linnaeus. Hence, the correct option is Abbreviated.

questions.question: Which are the terms interchangeable?
questions.question_raw: Which are the terms interchangeable?
questions.explanation: The characterization, identification, classification, and nomenclature are the processes that are basic to taxonomy as well as systematics. Hence, the correct option is Taxonomy and systematics.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > The Living World

questions.question:
Which is not the universal rule of nomenclature?
 Biological names are generally in Greek.
 The biological names are printed in italics.
 Genus name starts with a capital letter while the species name starts with small letters.
 The first word represents the genus and the second word represents species.
questions.question_raw:
Which is not the universal rule of nomenclature?

  1. Biological names are generally in Greek.
  2. The biological names are printed in italics.
  3. Genus name starts with a capital letter while the species name starts with small letters.
  4. The first word represents the genus and the second word represents species.
questions.explanation: According to the universal rule of nomenclature, the biological names are generally in Latin and written in italics. Hence, the correct option is Biological names are generally in Greek.

questions.question: The number of species that are known and described is:
questions.question_raw: The number of species that are known and described is:
questions.explanation: Each different kind of plant, animal, or organism represents a species. The number of species known and described ranges between 1.7-1.8 million. This refers to biodiversity or the number and types of organisms present on earth. Hence, the correct option is 1.7 - 1.8 million.

questions.question: The Darwin of the 20th century is:
questions.question_raw: The Darwin of the 20th century is:
questions.explanation: Born on 5 July 1904, in Kempten, Germany, ERNST MAYR, the Harvard University evolutionary biologist who has been called ‘The Darwin of the 20th century’, was one of the 100 greatest scientists of all time.

questions.question: A virus differ from a bacterium as it contains:
questions.question_raw: A virus differ from a bacterium as it contains:

options.label: D
options.text: DNA or RNA as genetic material with no ribosome

correct_option_texts: DNA or RNA as genetic material with no ribosome
questions.explanation: Virus differs from bacteria in terms of having DNA or RNA as genetic material with no ribosomes. The virus can replicate inside the host cell but become inactive outside the host cell.

questions.question: Wildlife is destroyed most when:
questions.question_raw: Wildlife is destroyed most when:

options.label: A
options.text: There is lack of proper care

questions.explanation: Wildlife is destroyed most when natural habitat is destroyed. The organisms survive and reproduce only in the natural habitat. The loss of habitat of wild animals is the main reason behind the extinction of organisms.

questions.question: Animals that have become extinct in India is _____.
questions.question_raw: Animals that have become extinct in India is _____.
questions.explanation: The animals that were present earlier but now not available are called extinct animals. In India, a number of animals have become extinct such as Cheetah.

questions.question: In which organism protoplasm and cellular structure is absent ______.
questions.question_raw: In which organism protoplasm and cellular structure is absent ______.
questions.explanation: The protoplasm and cellular structure are absent in Virus. A virus consists of genetic material (DNA or RNA) along with a capsule of protein without any cellular structure.

questions.question: Living organisms show internal growth due to the addition of materials and formation of cells inside the body. Such a method is called _______.
questions.question_raw: Living organisms show internal growth due to the addition of materials and formation of cells inside the body. Such a method is called _______.
questions.explanation: The method in which living organism show internal growth due to the addition of materials fand formation of cells inside the body is called intussusception.

questions.question: Example of interspecific hybrid is:
questions.question_raw: Example of interspecific hybrid is:
questions.explanation: Tigon and liger are produced by hybridization of tow interspecific species. Although hybridization generally takes place between the same species.

questions.question: A living organism is unexceptionally differentiated from a non-living structure on the basis of
questions.question_raw: A living organism is unexceptionally differentiated from a non-living structure on the basis of
questions.explanation: All organisms, from primitive prokaryotes to most advanced and complex eukaryotes, are able to sense and respond to environmental factors. The stimuli are perceived by sense organs in higher animals through sensory receptors e.g., eyes, ears, nose. Plants do not possess such sense organs. However, they do respond to external factors such as light, water, temperature, pollutants, other organisms, etc. Human beings have an additional facility of self-consciousness (awareness of self). Consciousness and response to stimuli are said to be the defining properties of living organisms.

questions.question: The number of categories in the hierarchical classification is ______.
questions.question_raw: The number of categories in the hierarchical classification is ______.
questions.explanation: Taxonomical studies of all known organisms have led to the development of common categories such as kingdom, phylum (for animals) or division (for plants), class, order, family, genus, and species. Hence, the correct option is 7.

questions.question: In a taxonomic hierarchy, the various categories are arranged in:
questions.question_raw: In a taxonomic hierarchy, the various categories are arranged in:
questions.explanation:
Kingdom $\rightarrow$ Phylum/Division $\rightarrow$ Class $\rightarrow$ Order $\rightarrow$ Family $\rightarrow$ Genus $\rightarrow$ Species.
Hence, the correct option is Descending order.

questions.question: Choose the correct sequence of taxonomic categories in descending order:
questions.question_raw: Choose the correct sequence of taxonomic categories in descending order:

options.label: A
options.text: Division - Order - Family - Class - Genus - Species

options.label: B
options.text: Division - Class - Order - Family - Genus - Species

options.label: C
options.text: Division - Family - Class - Order - Genus - Species

options.label: D
options.text: Division - Class - Order - Genus - Family - Species

correct_option_texts: Division - Class - Order - Family - Genus - Species
questions.explanation: The correct sequence of taxonomic categories form higher to lower are Division - Class - Order - Family - Genus - Species.

questions.question: A group of closely related families is called ______.
questions.question_raw: A group of closely related families is called ______.
questions.explanation: Class includes a group of related orders, Genus comprises a group of related species, species is a basic unit of classification. Hence, the correct option is Order.

questions.question: The highest categories of classification is ________.
questions.question_raw: The highest categories of classification is ________.
questions.explanation:
The taxonomic hierarchy Kingdom $\rightarrow$ Phylum/Division $\rightarrow$ Class $\rightarrow$ Order $\rightarrow$ Family $\rightarrow$ Genus $\rightarrow$ Species.
Hence, the correct option is the Kingdom.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > The Living World

questions.question: A group of closely related classes is called _______.
questions.question_raw: A group of closely related classes is called _______.
questions.explanation: Genus comprises a group of related species, Family has a group of related genera, the order has a group of related family. Hence, the correct option is Phylum.

questions.question: The basic unit of classification is ______.
questions.question_raw: The basic unit of classification is ______.
questions.explanation: The basic unit of classification is species. Species are the population of organisms that can interbreed among themselves to produce new organisms.

questions.question: Related genera belong to the same ________.
questions.question_raw: Related genera belong to the same ________.
questions.explanation: A family is a group of genera having some common properties. So, related genera belong to the same family.

questions.question: A fundamental category of classification ranking below a genus is_______.
questions.question_raw: A fundamental category of classification ranking below a genus is_______.
questions.explanation: The taxonomic hierarchy Kingdom $\rightarrow$ Phylum/Division $\rightarrow$ Class $\rightarrow$ Order $\rightarrow$ Family $\rightarrow$ Genus $\rightarrow$ Species. Hence, the correct option is Species.

questions.question: In a hierarchical system of classification of plants, which of the following taxonomic rank ends in ceae?
questions.question_raw: In a hierarchical system of classification of plants, which of the following taxonomic rank ends in ceae?
questions.explanation: Plant families like Convolvulaceae, Solanaceae are included in the order Polymoniales mainly based on the floral characters. Hence, the correct option is Family.

questions.question: Triticum aestivum belong to the family _______.
questions.question_raw: Triticum aestivum belong to the family _______.
questions.explanation: Triticum aestivum, common or bread wheat, is an annual grass in the Poaceae (grass family) native to the Mediterranean region and southwest Asia.

questions.question: A group of plants with similar traits of any rank is _______.
questions.question_raw: A group of plants with similar traits of any rank is _______.
questions.explanation: Each category in the taxonomic hierarchy represents the rank and is commonly termed as a taxon. Hence, the correct option is Taxon.

questions.question: Identify the correct binomial nomenclature of Mango:
questions.question_raw: Identify the correct binomial nomenclature of Mango:
questions.explanation: The biological name of an organism consists of the genus name and species name. the correct biological nomenclature of Mango is Mangifer indica Linn.

questions.question: What is the difference between the taxonomic categories of division and phylum?
questions.question_raw: What is the difference between the taxonomic categories of division and phylum?

options.label: A
options.text: The phylum is a more inclusive category

options.label: B
options.text: Division refers to plants whereas phylum refers to animals

options.label: C
options.text: Division is a more inclusive category

options.label: D
options.text: Phylum is more exclusive than division

correct_option_texts: Division refers to plants whereas phylum refers to animals
questions.explanation: In the case of plants, classes with a few similar characters are assigned to a higher category called division, whereas phylum was used to refer to animals. Hence, the correct option is Division refers to plants whereas phylum refers to animals.

questions.question: The housefly belongs to order ______.
questions.question_raw: The housefly belongs to order ______.
questions.explanation: Houseflies are insects of the order Diptera, the name is derived from the Greek di = two, and ptera = wings

questions.question:
Which is the incorrect statement from the following?
 Lion, tiger, leopard, and cat belong to the same genus.
 A family is a group of related genera with still less number of similarities as compared to genus and species.
 Taxonomic aids are the prime source of taxonomy, systematics, and future studies.
 Systematics takes into account the evolutionary relationship between organisms.
questions.question_raw:
Which is the incorrect statement from the following?

  1. Lion, tiger, leopard, and cat belong to the same genus.
  2. A family is a group of related genera with still less number of similarities as compared to genus and species.
  3. Taxonomic aids are the prime source of taxonomy, systematics, and future studies.
  4. Systematics takes into account the evolutionary relationship between organisms.
questions.explanation: Lion (Panthera leo), leopard (P. pardus), and tiger (P. Tigris) with several common features are all species of the genus Panthera. The genus Felisincludes cats. Hence, the correct option is Lion, tiger, leopard and cat belong to the same genus.

questions.question: What is the scientific name of wheat?
questions.question_raw: What is the scientific name of wheat?
questions.explanation: Wheat is a grass widely cultivated for its seed, a cereal grain which is a worldwide staple food. There are many species of wheat which together make up the genus Triticum; the most widely grown is common wheat (T. aestivum).

questions.question: A true species consist of a population:
questions.question_raw: A true species consist of a population:
questions.explanation:
As per the definition given by Ernst Mayr in 1964: "A species is a group of actually or potentially inbreeding populations that are reproductively isolated from other such groups."
A species may have subgroups, called subspecies or varieties, showing certain distinct features of their own.

questions.question: Systema Naturae is a book written by:
questions.question_raw: Systema Naturae is a book written by:
questions.explanation: ‘Systema Naturae’ is a book written by Carolus Linneaus. This book gives in-depth details of the classification system and binomial nomenclature of organisms.

questions.question: The scientific study of the diversity of organism and their evolutionary relationship is called:
questions.question_raw: The scientific study of the diversity of organism and their evolutionary relationship is called:
questions.explanation: Systematics is the scientific study of the diversity of organisms and their evolutionary relationship. Systematics is also known as taxonomy. It includes identification, nomenclature, and classification.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > The Living World

questions.question: Full form of ICBN is:
questions.question_raw: Full form of ICBN is:
questions.explanation: ICBN stands for the International code of botanical nomenclature that frames rules for the nomenclature of plants applicable all over the world.

questions.question: Which one is less general in characteristic as compared to the genus?
questions.question_raw: Which one is less general in characteristic as compared to the genus?
questions.explanation: Species is the lowest rank in the hierarchy. It means species contain the least general characters as compared to other categories.

questions.question: Identify from the following, category which has real existence.
questions.question_raw: Identify from the following, category which has real existence.
questions.explanation: Species

questions.question: Classification based on the evolutionary and genetic relationship is called:
questions.question_raw: Classification based on the evolutionary and genetic relationship is called:
questions.explanation: The classification of organisms on the basis of the evolutionary and genetic relationship is called a phylogenetic system of classification. The organisms are arranged according to their evolution on earth.

questions.question: The branch of study that deals with identification, nomenclature, and classification is called _______.
questions.question_raw: The branch of study that deals with identification, nomenclature, and classification is called _______.
questions.explanation: Taxonomy is the branch of biological science that deals with identification, nomenclature, and classification of organisms according to certain sets of rules.

questions.question: Which genus has a large number of closely related species?
questions.question_raw: Which genus has a large number of closely related species?
questions.explanation: A genus consists of a large number of closed related species. Genus Solanum consist of larger common species that include potato, tomato, brinjal, etc.

questions.question: Living organisms are classified because of:
questions.question_raw: Living organisms are classified because of:

options.label: B
options.text: Easy study and to keep records

options.label: C
options.text: To bring out similarities and to know habitat

questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: Carolus Linnaeus is associated with:
questions.question_raw: Carolus Linnaeus is associated with:
questions.explanation: Carolus Linnaeus is associated with Binomial nomenclature in which the name of the organism consists of the genus name and species name.

questions.question: Which one is an obligate category of classification?
questions.question_raw: Which one is an obligate category of classification?
questions.explanation: Family is the obligate category of classification as it forms the basic functional unit of classification. The family includes all the related species having similar characters.

questions.question: Plant and animal specimens may also be preserved as dry specimens in________.
questions.question_raw: Plant and animal specimens may also be preserved as dry specimens in________.
questions.explanation: Plant and animal specimens may also be preserved as dry specimens in museums. Hence, the correct option is the Museum.

questions.question: Which serve as quick referral systems in taxonomical studies?
questions.question_raw: Which serve as quick referral systems in taxonomical studies?
questions.explanation: Museums have collections of preserved plant and animal specimens for study and reference. Manuals are useful in providing information for the identification of names of species found in an area. Monographs contain information on anyone's taxon. Hence, the correct option is Herbarium.

questions.question: Specimens are preserved in the containers or jars in preservative solutions in ______.
questions.question_raw: Specimens are preserved in the containers or jars in preservative solutions in ______.
questions.explanation: Specimens are preserved in the containers or jars in preservative solutions in the museum.

questions.question: The existence of a wide range of different types of organisms in a given place at a given time is_______.
questions.question_raw: The existence of a wide range of different types of organisms in a given place at a given time is_______.
questions.explanation: The parks and zoo are the places where wild animals are kept in protected environments under human care. Museums have collections of preserved plant and animal specimens for study and reference. Hence, the correct option is Biodiversity.

questions.question: Larger animals like birds and mammals are usually stuffed and preserved in ______.
questions.question_raw: Larger animals like birds and mammals are usually stuffed and preserved in ______.
questions.explanation: Larger animals like birds and mammals are usually stuffed and preserved in the museum. Hence, the correct choice is the Museum.

questions.question: The famous botanical garden, Kew is located in ______.
questions.question_raw: The famous botanical garden, Kew is located in ______.
questions.explanation: Kew Gardens is a botanical garden in southwest London that houses the "largest and most diverse botanical and mycological collections in the world".

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > The Living World

questions.question: Herbarium is:
questions.question_raw: Herbarium is:

options.label: B
options.text: Collection of living plants for reference

options.label: D
options.text: Collection of preserved plant and animal specimen

questions.explanation: The herbarium is a storehouse of collected plant specimens that are dried, pressed and preserved on sheets. Hence, the correct option is Collection of dried plant specimens.

questions.question: National Botanical Research Institute is situated at ______.
questions.question_raw: National Botanical Research Institute is situated at ______.
questions.explanation: The famous botanical gardens are at Kew (England), Indian Botanical Garden, Howrah (India) and at National Botanical Research Institute, Lucknow (India). Hence, the correct choice is Lucknow.

questions.question: It is a handy book containing instructions of occurrence, collection, and identification of species found in a particular area that is known as ________.
questions.question_raw: It is a handy book containing instructions of occurrence, collection, and identification of species found in a particular area that is known as ________.
questions.explanation: Flora, manuals, monographs and catalogues are different means of recording descriptions. Manuals are useful in providing information for identification of names of species found in an area. Hence, the correct option is Manual.

questions.question: The taxonomical aid used for identification of plants and animals based on the similarities and dissimilarities is _______.
questions.question_raw: The taxonomical aid used for identification of plants and animals based on the similarities and dissimilarities is _______.
questions.explanation: Zoo and parks are the places where wild animals are kept in protected environments under human care. Museums have collections of preserved plant and animal specimens for study and reference. Hence, the correct option is Key.

questions.question: Each statement in the key is called ______.
questions.question_raw: Each statement in the key is called ______.
questions.explanation: The keys are based on the contrasting characters generally in a pair called a couplet. Manuals are useful in providing information for identification of names of species found in an area. Monographs contain information on anyone taxon. Hence, the correct option is Lead.

questions.question: What contains the actual account of habitat and distribution of plants of a given area?
questions.question_raw: What contains the actual account of habitat and distribution of plants of a given area?
questions.explanation: Manuals are useful in providing information for the identification of names of species found in an area. Monographs contain information on anyone taxon. Catalogue is also another means of recording descriptions which helps incorrect identification. Hence, the correct option is Flora.

questions.question: National Botanical Institute is located in ________.
questions.question_raw: National Botanical Institute is located in ________.
questions.explanation: The national botanical research institute is located in Lucknow (UP). This is one of the largest botanical research institutes in the world housing thousands of plants.

questions.question: Rajaji national park is in ______.
questions.question_raw: Rajaji national park is in ______.
questions.explanation: Rajaji national park is an Indian national park and tiger reserve that encompasses the Shivaliks, near the foothill of the Himalayas in Uttrakhand.

questions.question: Lions are found in:
questions.question_raw: Lions are found in:
questions.explanation: In India, lions are found in Gir forests located in the state of Gujarat, India. It is the sole home of the Asiatic lions and is considered to be one of the most important protected areas in Asia.

questions.question: Largest Botanical Garden in the world is:
questions.question_raw: Largest Botanical Garden in the world is:
questions.explanation:
The Royal Botanical Garden, Kew (located in London, United Kingdom) is the largest and most renowned botanical garden in the world. It covers approximately 330 acres and houses one of the most extensive and diverse collections of living plants and preserved plant specimens. Kew Gardens is also a leading center for botanical research and conservation.
It is also a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a leading center for botanical research and conservation.

questions.question: List of alternate characters arranged in such a manner that by selection and elimination an organism can easily be identified is______.
questions.question_raw: List of alternate characters arranged in such a manner that by selection and elimination an organism can easily be identified is______.
questions.explanation: Key is the list of alternate characters arranged in such a manner that by selection and elimination an organism can easily be identified. It is an important tool for the classification of the organism.

questions.question: The first Biosphere Reserve established in India is:
questions.question_raw: The first Biosphere Reserve established in India is:
questions.explanation: The Nilgiri Biosphere reserve is the first biosphere reserve established in India. Biosphere reserves are set to protect the flora and fauna of a particular area that houses a large number of wild animals.

questions.question: A specified area in which multiple uses of the land is permitted by dividing into zones, each for a particular activity is called ________.
questions.question_raw: A specified area in which multiple uses of the land is permitted by dividing into zones, each for a particular activity is called ________.
questions.explanation: A biosphere reserve is divided into a number of zones, where different kinds of activities are allowed. In the outer zone, agriculture and other activities are allowed but in the central or core zone, no human activities are allowed.

questions.question: Preservative liquid used for storing fleshy organs is
questions.question_raw: Preservative liquid used for storing fleshy organs is
questions.explanation: In a biological laboratory, formalin is used as a preservative liquid used for storing fleshy organs.

questions.question: It is a handy book containing instructions as to the occurrence, collection, and identification of species found in a particular area.
questions.question_raw: It is a handy book containing instructions as to the occurrence, collection, and identification of species found in a particular area.
questions.explanation: Manual is a handy book containing instructions as to the occurrence, collection, and identification of species found in a particular area. It serves as an important tool for the classification of organisms.
